records.ety: [Akin to Sw. sacka to settle, sink down, LG. sacken, D. zakken. Cf. Sink, v. i.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sagged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sagging (?).]

defs: To sink, in the middle, by its weight or under applied pressure, below a horizontal line or plane; as, a line or cable supported by its ends sags, though tightly drawn; the floor of a room sags; hence, to lean, give way, or settle from a vertical position; as, a building may sag one way or another; a door sags on its hinges.
Fig.: To lose firmness or elasticity; to sink; to droop; to flag; to bend; to yield, as the mind or spirits, under the pressure of care, trouble, doubt, or the like; to be unsettled or unbalanced.
quotes: The mind I sway by, and the heart I bear, Shall never sag with doubt nor shake with fear.

defs: To loiter in walking; to idle along; to drag or droop heavily.
To cause to bend or give way; to load.
State of sinking or bending; sagging.
